title: Humboltia erinacea
intro: Humboldtia erinacea, also known as the 'Erinacea tree', is a plant species native to the Western Ghats in India, belonging to the family Sapindaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Humboldtia erinacea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into various topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
